summary: This fragment analyzes the legal implications and case law surrounding stop and frisk in the context of Criminal Procedure.
key_points: Legal scholars debate the proper scope and application of stop and frisk.
Historical evolution shows shifts in judicial interpretation over time.
Competing theories offer different frameworks for analysis.
Empirical studies examine the real-world impact of this doctrine.
Comparative analysis with other jurisdictions reveals variations.
Ongoing litigation continues to shape the boundaries of this area.

philosophy_guard.statement: This fragment describes what IS - the existing legal framework, procedures, and analytical perspectives. It does not advocate for what OUGHT to be.
